purpose: Typed, evidence-backed model of how Robeul actually works with Claude. Supersedes the prose docs (ROBEUL-PROFILE.md, WINS-AND-FAILS.md). Every parameter carries evidence: a measured count from the corpus and/or a real (redacted) quote. Authored from a deterministic mine — no abstract guessing.
method: Deterministic mine (model/mine_corpus.py + model/extract_pairs.py) over 1359 Robeul-driven Claude Code sessions + 490 vault chats across rentamac/tl-vps/lgj-vm. Sub-agent (Claude-to-Claude) sessions and compaction/hook noise were excluded. 95 high-precision real corrections were extracted and bucketed. Secrets scrubbed. Corpus is complete for the live fleet (tl-scraper + robeul-server decommissioned 2026-06-16).

interaction_shape.claim: Short directive in, long autonomous run expected.
interaction_shape.evidence: avg 2.4 user turns vs 31.5 tool calls per session; heavy use of ScheduleWakeup (222), Workflow (60), Agent (271), TaskCreate/Update (1115).
interaction_shape.implication: He hands a terse goal and expects the agent to run far without check-ins. Pausing to ask, or stopping early, reads as failure. But running far in the WRONG direction is the worst outcome — verify the target first, then go long.

voice_decode.note: How his words map to state. Quotes are real, redacted.

terse_lowercase_typo_directive.means: Normal operating mode. He typed fast; intent over grammar. Execute, don't ask him to clarify spelling.

english_profanity.means: Trust slipping. You drifted, wasted time, or claimed done falsely. Stop, admit, fix the one thing.
english_profanity.example: "what the fuck man!!! stop wasting my times"

romanized_bengali_profanity.means: PEAK anger — trust broken (the rarest, sharpest signal: native-tongue switch).
romanized_bengali_profanity.example: "eii [REDACTED] li bal kortesos vodar bal somossa ki", "memory takhte paroah naa" (= you don't even keep this domain's memory)
romanized_bengali_profanity.protocol: Kill the running agent/process, admit the specific mistake in one line, revert out-of-scope changes, execute the single minimal real action. Do NOT explain or defend.

time_framing.means: Time is his primary failure metric. 'How many hours', 'wasting my time' = you cost him hours with nothing shipped.
time_framing.example: "You're waste my how many Hours?", "7 hours [task]"

discipline_demand.means: He is invoking the rules explicitly; zero tolerance left.
discipline_demand.example: "be very disciplined or get lost", "don't rolling me" (= stop redirecting/bouncing me, just do it)

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "Nope the wrong built claudes fake execution"; "you were gone ahead and back said done"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Never say done/working until you ran the real path THIS session and can quote literal proof inline (row counts, actual output — not HTTP 200). Not run -> 'not verified'. Blocked -> 'NOT DONE — blocked by X'.
failure_modes_ranked.frequency_caveat: Proxy = assistant claimed done AND a correction/failure followed. The strongest, most repeated complaint across the corpus.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "we don't done yet please stop your over doing over stepping behaviour"; "who gave you that permission to use new source for dedupe"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Build ONLY the named noun + path. Touch only the files/paths he named. On 'remove/simplify' do the single smallest step and DELETE scope — never add a layer, never a menu. Anything unnamed = ask in one line first.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "stop wasting my times"; "are you able to productional or keep continueing my time wasting?"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Optimize for his hours, not yours. Pin ONE workspace, take the smallest correct action, finish + deploy. Don't explore, narrate, or re-derive what's already established.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "wtf you're doing it again with the wrong emails i said sheets listes domain isn't valid"; "Flagging your unexecutions I asked you to check and compare with apollo"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Follow HIS documented pattern verbatim. If he said apollo.io, do apollo.io. Re-read his exact words before acting; repeating a corrected mistake is the trust-killer.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "stop old memory review i accessed it try real"; "fuck your brain" (got same wrong result twice)
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Memory/brain is point-in-time and often stale. Load LIVE state (config/logs/DB/curl) before answering or coding. The moment you're about to assume a path/fact/capability, read it instead. Re-confirm the infra map; it changes under you.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "wait don't move wrong path check the dedicated sandbox running inside tl vps for outboundrix"; "which sheet we used before?"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Confirm WHICH app/repo/server/sheet/DB before touching it. One workspace per task, pinned. Verify the target exists and is the right one — wrong-target work is wasted hours.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "What do you think by this kind of options every time? The fucking shit."; "stop bargaining with me"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: No A/B/C menus, no capacity-math, no 'want me to also'. Pick the best path and execute. Needing input = ONE line, the ask IS the message. Never argue/defend a choice — act.

failure_modes_ranked.real_quote: "the most hardest part is youre always working with guess and always doing what you want"; "stop your guessing behavior Make sure proper brain"
failure_modes_ranked.rule: Don't guess capabilities, paths, or results. Check first. If you can't verify, say so explicitly rather than asserting.

domain_ownership.note: Where his work actually goes, by session volume — drives which sub-agents the core orchestrator needs first.

outboundrix.note: Outboundrix-Dev (453) + web monorepo (133); cold-email + Apollo-prospecting SaaS. By far the heaviest. Benchmark = Apollo.io + Instantly.ai.

brain.note: brain-api (92) + brain-assistant (43) + brain-stack/llm/quartz/workspaces; the AI control plane itself

fleet_ops.note: root (127) + rentamac (104); SSH/infra/host ops on the control plane and remote boxes

tool_profile.interpretation: Operator/dev work, not chat. He runs the agent in bypassPermissions/auto modes (203 of 277 mode-stamped turns) and leans on scheduling + workflows + sub-agents for set-and-forget autonomy. Heavy Bash = real execution expected, not advice.

decision_style.directive_density: Very high — short asks, large expected output.
decision_style.autonomy_tolerance: High on execution, LOW on scope drift. Run long, but never widen the scope he set.
decision_style.verification_demand: Absolute. Proof or 'not verified'. He has been burned by false-done ~35% of sessions.
decision_style.patience_for_options: Zero. Menus and bargaining trigger anger.

anger_base_rate.note: Rare but sharp; almost always preceded by false-done, overstepping, or time-waste. Romanized Bengali = the ceiling.

do: Restate his spec in one line, then build ONLY that.
Load live state before acting; verify before claiming.
Run long and autonomously once the target is confirmed correct.
Lead with the result; <=3 sentences; silence between tool calls.
On 'remove/simplify': delete scope, smallest step, no new layer.
Quote literal proof (row counts, real output) when reporting done.
dont: Don't say done without running the real path this session.
Don't add anything he didn't name.
Don't present option menus or bargain — pick and execute.
Don't trust stale memory/brain over live state.
Don't repeat a mistake he already corrected.
Don't redirect/'roll' him — confirm and proceed.
Don't explain or defend when he's angry — kill, admit, fix.
